segments.text: in honor of women's history month we
wanted to highlight some incredible
women whose work led to the Innovative
technology we use today
did you know that the first computer
programmer was a woman widely known as
the enchantess of numbers Ada lovelace's
work on Charles babbage's analytical
engine played a major influence on Alan
turning's work on the first modern
computer in the 1940s inventor of one of
the first link editors Grace Hopper was
well known for her pioneering machine
independent programming languages her
work led to the development of cobalt a
programming language we still use today
an actress during Hollywood's golden age
and esteemed mathematician Hedy Lamarr
developed a frequency hopping technology
laying the foundation for Wi-Fi and
other Wireless Communications like GPS
and Bluetooth Mary Allen Wilkes worked
closely designing the software for the
link one of the earliest systems of an
interactive personal computer in 1965
she took the link home making her the
first ever home computer user a rocket
scientist and a proud advocate for
intersectional diversity in stem Annie
Easley's 34-year career saw the
development of important computer code
spanning Alternative Energy solarwind
projects and the 1997 flight to Saturn
of the Cassini probe

fullText: in honor of women's history month we wanted to highlight some incredible women whose work led to the Innovative technology we use today did you know that the first computer programmer was a woman widely known as the enchantess of numbers Ada lovelace's work on Charles babbage's analytical engine played a major influence on Alan turning's work on the first modern computer in the 1940s inventor of one of the first link editors Grace Hopper was well known for her pioneering machine independent programming languages her work led to the development of cobalt a programming language we still use today an actress during Hollywood's golden age and esteemed mathematician Hedy Lamarr developed a frequency hopping technology laying the foundation for Wi-Fi and other Wireless Communications like GPS and Bluetooth Mary Allen Wilkes worked closely designing the software for the link one of the earliest systems of an interactive personal computer in 1965 she took the link home making her the first ever home computer user a rocket scientist and a proud advocate for intersectional diversity in stem Annie Easley's 34-year career saw the development of important computer code spanning Alternative Energy solarwind projects and the 1997 flight to Saturn of the Cassini probe
